sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: FC St. Pauli, sitting at 14th in the league with 31 points, host VfB Stuttgart, who are positioned 10th with 44 points, in Round 32 of the Bundesliga at the iconic Millerntor-Stadion in Hamburg - St. Pauli. This matchup promises to be intriguing as both teams are fighting for better standings with just a few games left in the season. The referee, Florian Exner, is known for averaging 4.2 yellow cards per game, which could influence the physicality of the match.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last encounter on December 21, 2024, FC St. Pauli secured a 0-1 victory over VfB Stuttgart, with goalkeeper Nikola Vasilj earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive 8.2 Sofascore rating. Historically, over the last 5 matchups, VfB Stuttgart has dominated with 3 wins, while FC St. Pauli managed only 1 win, and 1 game ended in a draw. This suggests Stuttgart has the upper hand in recent history, but St. Pauli’s latest win could boost their confidence.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - FC St. Pauli
sections.text: For FC St. Pauli, forward Morgan Guilavogui stands out with 5 goals and an average Sofascore rating of 7.0, though he’s slightly underperformed his expected goals of 6.19. Another player to keep an eye on is Noah Weißhaupt, ranked 5th in the league for fouls drawn per game at 2.4, which could disrupt Stuttgart’s rhythm. Additionally, Johannes Eggestein contributes with 3 goals and 4 assists, making him a creative force despite underperforming his 4.72 expected goals.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - VfB Stuttgart
sections.text: VfB Stuttgart boasts firepower in Ermedin Demirović, who leads their scoring with 13 goals against an expected 12.69, and is in form with 3 goals in his last 5 matches. Midfielder Angelo Stiller, with an average rating of 7.4 and a league-leading 8.60 expected assists, including 3 assists in his last 5 games, is another standout ranked highly in the league for playmaking. Also, defender Finn Jeltsch impresses with a 69.4% ground duels won rate, ranking 5th league-wide, providing defensive stability.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: FC St. Pauli are on a streak of no losses in their last 4 matches, which could give them a psychological edge at home. However, they’ve conceded first in 4 of their last 5 games, indicating defensive vulnerabilities early on. VfB Stuttgart, on the other hand, have gone 3 games without a win and failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 3 matches, suggesting defensive frailties. Interestingly, 8 of St. Pauli’s last 10 home games have seen under 2.5 goals, while Stuttgart’s away games often feature over 2.5 goals in 4 of their last 5.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Insights
sections.text: FC St. Pauli struggle offensively, ranking 18th in total goals scored with just 26, and converting only 23.33% of big chances, also ranking 18th. However, their defense is solid, conceding only 37 goals, ranking 2nd for the least goals conceded. VfB Stuttgart shows more attacking prowess, especially late in games, with 29.41% of their goals (13 goals) scored between the 76th and 90th minutes. Both teams have a trend of matches with less than 10.5 corners in 4 of their last 5 combined games, hinting at a potentially low-corner encounter.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players Impact
sections.text: FC St. Pauli will miss key defender Karol Mets, who ranks 3rd in the league with a 70.6% aerial duels won rate, potentially weakening their backline against headers. They also lack midfielder Jackson Irvine, who has 6 assists this season, impacting their creativity. VfB Stuttgart are without midfielder Enzo Millot, who has 5 goals and 4 assists, which could hurt their midfield dynamism. These absences might tilt the balance slightly, depending on tactical adjustments.

sections.subtitle: Standout Historical Performer
sections.text: In the last 5 matchups between these teams, VfB Stuttgart’s Chris Führich has been a standout performer with an average Sofascore rating of 7.275 and 2 goals scored. Although he’s only started 1 of those games, his impact when on the pitch has been notable. His current season stats show 2 goals and 2 assists, and he’ll likely be a threat again if given space by St. Pauli’s defense.
